recent_news.title: Pope Leo criticises Equatorial Guinea prisons as he winds up Africa tour
recent_news.summary: The Pope gives a frank and passionate speech, the likes of which have characterised his tour of Africa.
recent_news.body_text:
Pope Leo has criticised the treatment of prisoners in Equatorial Guinea, ahead of a visit to a jail known for its dire conditions.
He addressed the Central African nation on Wednesday at a Mass that drew some 100,000 people, including Teodoro Obiang Nguema Mbasogo, the country's leader and the world's long-serving president.

recent_news.title: What is Strategic Rivalry? Why Should We Care?
recent_news.summary: The states most likely to draw America into its next major crisis or war are not unknowns. They are the usual suspects: The same handful of states that have threatened the United States repeatedly across decades. Interstate rivals have caused roughly 80 percent of history’s wars and the odds of any given rivalry ending peacefully are little better than a coin toss. The regimental commander agreed. The surprise assault broke the Vietnamese defense. general_news.title: How lessons from Iran war could shape mainland China’s calculus on Taiwan
general_news.summary: Whether it wraps up quickly or drags on, the repercussions of the US-Israeli war on Iran will echo for years, reshaping warfare, geopolitics, energy security and global perceptions of American tactical and strategic power. In the first of a three-part series, Mark Magnier looks at how the Iran war may alter Beijing’s approach to potential conflict over Taiwan, asymmetrical weaponry and the United States as an adversary. The US military is formidable, well-disciplined, projects deadly force...
